audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel chapter 20, first-pass restored draft
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for David's danger assessment, Jonathan's changing belief, new-moon meal, covenant reciprocity, intergenerational protection, coded arrow signal, misogynistic insult, spear attack against Jonathan, child messenger, kissing, weeping, and queer-erasure/misuse.

verses.restored: David fled from Naioth in Ramah, came before Jonathan, and said, What have I done? What is my guilt and what is my sin before your father, that he seeks my life?
Jonathan said to him, Far be it! You shall not die. Look, my father does nothing great or small without uncovering my ear. Why would my father hide this matter from me? It is not so.

David swore again and said, Your father surely knows I have found favor in your eyes, and he says, Let Jonathan not know this lest he be grieved. Yet as YHWH lives and as your life lives, there is but a step between me and death.
Jonathan said to David, Whatever your life says, I will do for you.
David said to Jonathan, Look, new moon is tomorrow, and I should sit with king to eat. Send me away, and I will hide in field until evening of third day.
If your father misses me, say, David earnestly asked leave from me to run to Bethlehem his city, because yearly sacrifice is there for all clan.
If he says, Good, there is peace for your servant. But if his anger burns, know that harm is determined by him.
Deal in covenant-kindness with your servant, for into covenant of YHWH you brought your servant with you. But if there is guilt in me, put me to death yourself; why bring me to your father?
Jonathan said, Far be it from you! If I certainly knew harm was determined by my father to come upon you, would I not tell you?
David said to Jonathan, Who will tell me if your father answers you harshly?
Jonathan said to David, Come, let us go out to field. They both went out to field.
Jonathan said to David, By YHWH Divine of Israel: when I search out my father about this time tomorrow or third day, if there is good toward David, will I not send to you and uncover your ear?

Thus may YHWH do to Jonathan and thus add if harm seems good to my father against you and I do not uncover your ear and send you away, so you may go in peace. May YHWH be with you as he was with my father.
And if I am still alive, will you not show me covenant-kindness of YHWH, so I do not die?
Do not cut off your covenant-kindness from my house forever, not even when YHWH cuts off each enemy of David from face of ground.
Jonathan cut covenant with house of David, saying, May YHWH seek it from hand of David's enemies.
Jonathan made David swear again by his love for him, because he loved him as he loved his own life.
Jonathan said to him, Tomorrow is new moon, and you will be missed because your seat will be empty.
On third day go down quickly and come to place where you hid on day of deed, and remain beside stone Ezel.
I will shoot three arrows to its side, as though shooting at a target.
Look, I will send boy, saying, Go find arrows. If I expressly say to boy, Look, arrows are on this side of you; take them, then come, for there is peace for you and no matter, as YHWH lives.
But if I say to youth, Look, arrows are beyond you, go, for YHWH has sent you away.
As for matter we spoke, I and you, look, YHWH is between me and you forever.
David hid in field. New moon came, and king sat at food to eat.
King sat upon his seat as time after time, seat by wall. Jonathan arose, Abner sat beside Saul, and David's place was empty.
Saul spoke nothing that day, for he said, It is an accident; he is not clean, surely not clean.
On day after new moon, second day, David's place was empty. Saul said to Jonathan his son, Why has son of Jesse not come to food either yesterday or today?
Jonathan answered Saul, David earnestly asked leave from me to Bethlehem.

He said, Send me away, please, because our clan has sacrifice in city and my brother commanded me. Now if I have found favor in your eyes, let me slip away, please, and see my brothers. Therefore he has not come to table of king.
Saul's anger burned against Jonathan. He said to him, Son of a perverse rebellious woman! Do I not know you choose son of Jesse to your shame and shame of nakedness of your mother?
For all days son of Jesse lives upon ground, you and your kingdom shall not be established. Now send and take him to me, because he is a son of death.
Jonathan answered Saul his father and said to him, Why should he be put to death? What has he done?
Saul hurled spear at him to strike him. Jonathan knew that it was determined by his father to put David to death.
Jonathan rose from table in burning anger and did not eat food on second day of new moon, because he grieved for David and because his father had humiliated him.
In morning Jonathan went out to field at appointment with David, and a small boy was with him.
He said to boy, Run, find arrows I shoot. Boy ran, and he shot arrow beyond him.
When boy came to place of arrow Jonathan shot, Jonathan called after boy and said, Is not arrow beyond you?
Jonathan called after boy, Hurry, be quick, do not stand. Jonathan's boy gathered arrows and came to his master.
Boy knew nothing; only Jonathan and David knew matter.
Jonathan gave his weapons to boy and said to him, Go, bring them to city.
Boy went. David rose from beside south, fell upon his face to ground, and bowed three times. They kissed one another and wept with one another, David exceeding.

Jonathan said to David, Go in peace, since we both swore in name of YHWH, saying, YHWH shall be between me and you and between my seed and your seed forever. David arose and went, and Jonathan entered city.

translation_notes: David directly names the threat to his life. Survivors may perceive danger before relatives or insiders are ready to believe them.
Jonathan initially trusts his father's usual disclosure, then tests the claim and changes his understanding. Loyalty and love require responsiveness to new evidence.
The covenant between David and Jonathan is reciprocal, intimate, political, and intergenerational. It may be explored without erasing queer readings or forcing a single modern identity label.
David's request that Jonathan kill him if guilty reflects extreme danger and ancient honor logic; it is not a model for suicide, assisted killing, or bypassing due process.
The new-moon plan uses concealment and coded signals for survival. People escaping violence are not morally required to disclose their location.
Saul's insult attacks Jonathan through misogynistic shaming of his mother and sexualized family honor. The unnamed mother is not responsible for the conflict.
Saul's claim that David threatens Jonathan's kingdom reveals dynastic fear. Political succession does not justify murder.
Saul hurls a spear at his own son. Jonathan's refusal to eat is grief and protest, not proof that fasting itself is spiritually superior.
The boy is used as an unwitting messenger; children should not be placed in dangerous adult conflicts or made carriers of secrets they cannot consent to.
The final kissing, weeping, oath, and separation express profound attachment. Their love is not shameful and must not be weaponized against LGBTQ people, friendship, masculinity, or faith.
'YHWH between us' makes the covenant accountable beyond immediate advantage; it does not make either man beyond later moral scrutiny.
safety_note: This chapter centers survival under domestic and political violence. Do not demand that endangered people reveal hiding places, reconcile with an armed abuser, or accept a broken family system for the sake of loyalty. Saul's misogynistic insult and spear attack are abuse. David and Jonathan's love must not be erased, mocked, sexualized against their will, or weaponized against queer people. Their death-language is not permission for suicide, honor killing, or private execution.
